title: Net-New Story Gap Review — 2026-05-16 (HRREC-82977)
content:
Executive summary (for PM)

  • Run tier: A (full contract). Companion: manifest-only WhatsApp frozen corpus 2026-05-16 — no live Jira for WhatsApp companion keys this run.
  • Scope: 44 Stories under HRREC-82977; skipped doc-writer keys HRREC-90852 (AG:), HRREC-90853 (RN:). Peanut MCP returned Jira fetch errors for every collectBugData attempt — Dev lens documents that honestly.

Top 5 gaps (epic)

  • Placeholder security refactor HRREC-92022 lacks acceptance while HRREC-91975 depends on notification routing — align OE before build commitment.
  • Cross-surface logging HRREC-92035 bundles Activity Stream, Timeline, and reporting without a per-surface matrix — split or attach traceability before QA load explodes.
  • Bounce/recruiter-visible error paths HRREC-92003 conflict with Deployment Agent silence on in-product bounce UX — schedule OE copy mapping for SES outcomes.
  • Draft persistence vs “no backend drafts” tension spans HRREC-91986, HRREC-91995, and HRREC-91996 — decide collapse/navigation behaviour once.
  • XO metadata searches often returned generic relationships for plain-text queries such as two way email and Recruiting Email — engineering should publish canonical class/REST names for gap reviews.

Top 5 strengths (epic)

  • Initialization pair HRREC-91946/91948 grounds SSP placement, masking, and responsive width with explicit Figma and scenario detail.
  • Compose stack HRREC-91987–91994 chains header fields, RTE, validation, discard, and send with explicit UI-only vs REST boundaries.
  • Agency security cluster HRREC-92007–92008 names forbidden cross-thread access — rare explicit authz language for a recruiting UI epic.
  • Operational realism stories HRREC-92003–92005 and HRREC-92010 translate SES and merge edge cases into recruiter-visible messaging.
  • Tenant lifecycle HRREC-92012 aligns with documented Edit Tenant Setup – Recruiting conversation day settings from Deployment Agent.

Epic-level notes

  • Overlapping expand/collapse stories (91948, 91980, 92001) should share one design token appendix to avoid conflicting widths in QA.
  • Send path split HRREC-91994 vs HRREC-92036 (attachments) must keep polling, duplicate-send prevention, and Blobatory payload docs synchronized.
  • Reporting regression HRREC-92013 should either link to a living test matrix or be demoted to Task/Spike — current text is milestone tone, not acceptance-ready.
  • Notification + My Conversations + email redirect triangle (91975, Deployment Agent B) needs a single one-page decision memo to prevent duplicate alerts.

Cross-initiative pattern hints (WhatsApp — inspiration only)

Frozen pattern corpus — snapshot_as_of 2026-05-16 in docs/initiatives/two-way-email/reference/WHATSAPP_COMPANION_CORPUS_SNAPSHOT.md (66 manifest keys). No live Jira pull for WhatsApp companion this run (manifest-only). Theme anchors use Captured excerpts only — not current WhatsApp backlog.

Same surface, different channel: WhatsApp and email are both inbound/outbound Recruiting comms, but consent, templates, media, delivery semantics, and failure modes differ. Different backend / OE: WhatsApp partner delivery is often Twilio-oriented in internal discussions; two-way email uses Workday’s email integration path. Bugs = delivery pain signals, not email defects. Inspiration only — never treat WhatsApp issues as proof email is broken.

  • HRREC-84403 / HRREC-89856 (template preview vs send hydration) — Consider for 2WE if MB preview and sent email bodies always resolve tokens under the same recruiter security context?
  • HRREC-90268 / HRREC-92062 (disable send / typing while in flight) — Consider for 2WE if email compose should disable Send and buffer typing during slow REST responses?
  • HRREC-89979 (failures surfacing late) — Consider for 2WE if recruiter-visible errors appear when opening a thread, not only after pressing Send?
  • HRREC-90306 / HRREC-84389 (merge identity) — Consider for 2WE if candidate merge could mis-attribute email threads or From addresses?
  • HRREC-89899 (broken consent chrome when tenant text missing) — Consider for 2WE if email opt-in UI hides broken links when tenant notification text is incomplete?
Story Gap score (%) PM lens QA lens Dev lens Verdict Suggested missing BDD (Given/When/Then)
HRREC-91946
[Initialisation] Recruiter can see Email Task on SSP (XO Only)		 38% — PM and Jira are clear on ordering; QA calls out masking matrix; Dev lens shows generic XO hits, not email-task wiring.
  • Jira — Strong scenario coverage for task order, masking when job application is masked, and combined masked icon across channels.
  • Salomon — SSP visibility still depends on separate Candidate Notes vs Job Application Notes domains—confirm copy does not imply access the recruiter lacks.
  • Deployment Agent — Channel rollout remains Edit Tenant Setup–Recruiting plus domain policies; plan which domains gate the new email task before UAT.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — Snapshot excerpt HRREC-84555 (profile entry icon when opted in) — consider for 2WE if the email task should hide when consent or tenant email channel is off; email uses different consent surface than WhatsApp.
  • Jira — Risk: recruiter sees a masked icon but one channel is actually visible elsewhere—watch inconsistent masking across email vs SMS/WhatsApp.
  • Salomon — Prior conversational panel glitches when tenant SMS redirect conflicts with two-way messaging suggest retesting SSP after unrelated tenant comms changes.
  • XO MCP — Searched conversational email; results were generic secured-instance relationships, not the task derivation you described.
  • XO MCP — risk: Ask engineering which XO task layout version lands the Conversational Email task relative to Notes for each breakpoint.
  • Peanut — collectBugData failed (Jira fetch error in MCP this run)—no repo or similar-issue signal.
  • Finding: Initialization story is scenario-rich; main residual risk is XO layout wiring vs generic search hits.
  • Recommended next step: Run one SSP walkthrough on a tenant with split Notes vs Job Application Notes domains before calling this slice done.

Scenario: Masked application with partial domain access

Given a recruiter has Job Application Notes but not Candidate Notes domain access
When they open the candidate profile with a masked application
Then the email task affordance matches the same masking rules as SMS/WhatsApp and does not leak thread text.
HRREC-91948
[Initialisation] Enabled SSP Growth/Expansion		 42% — UX numbers settled in Q and A; Dev lens only surfaced WATS side-panel tests; PM leaves product question on grown-state persistence.
  • Jira — Widths and XL overlay are specified; Scenario 4 explicitly flags open product question on reopen behaviour after grow.
  • Salomon — UI Platform docs distinguish modal vs side-panel focus and refresh—use them when deciding persist-vs-reset on navigation.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-84533 excerpt (countdown accuracy on refresh) — consider for 2WE if any expiry or reply-window UI for email must refresh timers when the panel reloads; channel timing semantics differ.
  • Jira — Recruiters may lose in-flight email compose if grow state resets unpredictably—capture expected behaviour for close/reopen and task switch.
  • Salomon — Collaboration panel push vs overlay discussions (HRRECUI-459) are a precedent for desktop breakpoint edge cases.
  • XO MCP — Searched sliding side panel; hits are WATS layout and SMS/WhatsApp sliding panel tests—automation exists, not product answers for email grow state.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Good visual spec with an intentional open point on whether grown layout survives navigation.
  • Recommended next step: Resolve Scenario 4 with UX + eng in one decision memo, then fold into discard/navigation sibling stories.

Scenario: Task switch after grow

Given the recruiter expanded the panel to the wide email layout
When they switch to another SSP task and back
Then the story documents whether width resets or persists so QA can script it once.
HRREC-91974
PH: Recruiter sees Empty state of email task if opened		 35% — Jira names REST label work; QA informed by WhatsApp empty-state precedent; XO found conversationContextInstance artefacts.
  • Jira — Clear empty-state UI with Compose/New/Grow; calls out header label check with PM contact.
  • Salomon — WhatsApp EA empty-state pattern (HRREC-84383) and blank conversational panel issues suggest parallel negative tests.
  • Jira — Watch header wording vs accessibility when no messages exist; buttons are no-op until later stories—recruiters may think the product is broken.
  • Salomon — Prior instability when tenant messaging redirect conflicts with two-way flows—retest empty state after comms admin changes.
  • XO MCP — Searched conversationContextInstance; surfaced service representation and WATS REST tests—adjacent to the GET endpoint named in Jira.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Solid slice for first-run experience with explicit REST label dependencies.
  • Recommended next step: Confirm which REST version recruiters hit in dev SUV matches the label container instance named in Jira before freezing strings.

Scenario: No conversation yet

Given no thread exists for the job application
When the recruiter opens the email task
Then empty-state graphic and actions match Figma and no phantom draft appears.
HRREC-91975
PH: Recruiter receives a notification		 55% — Jira assumes framework routing; Deployment Agent says My Conversations today is SMS-primary; tension for email notifications.
  • Jira — Covers participant routing, deep link into SSP thread, and optional standard email alert when admin routes Candidate SMS/Email Messages.
  • Deployment Agent — My Conversations currently emphasises SMS conversational paths; bell vs email notifications remain tenant choices in Notifications setup.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — WhatsApp security-domain gating (internal articles referenced in Salomon) — consider for 2WE if email replies should bypass My Conversations when domains differ from SMS; backend differs from Twilio-oriented WhatsApp.
  • Jira — Recruiters without domain access may see bell-only signals—exercise both participant and non-participant recruiters.
  • Deployment Agent — Risk of duplicate noise if both bell and email notifications fire for the same reply—decide default for 2WE.
  • XO MCP — Searched My Conversations; surfaced WATS REST scenario and security-policy loops wiring recruiter groups to the My Conversations domain.
  • XO MCP — risk: HRREC-92022 notes an XO refactor—confirm notification event names land where My Conversations listeners expect them for email.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Functional story hinges on tenant notification routing that still skews SMS in admin guidance.
  • Recommended next step: Hold a 30-minute OE alignment on whether email replies mirror SMS My Conversations suppression or reuse bell defaults.

Scenario: Participant vs non-participant

Given an inbound email reply is ingested
When the primary recruiter lacks the same security as a secondary watcher
Then each person gets an in-product or email notification path consistent with tenant notifications configuration.
HRREC-91978
PH: Admin can enable or disable 2-way email as a comms channel		 48% — Jira is high-level; DA gives Recruiting + Notifications tasks; XO search returned zero hits for the plain-text query two way email.
  • Jira — States auditable enable/disable with recruiter-visible consequences—needs concrete admin task names in AC.
  • Deployment Agent — Channels tie to Edit Tenant Setup – Recruiting and Notifications, plus security domains such as Manage: Candidate SMS Conversations for SMS—expect parallel domain work for email.
  • Jira — Risk that disable still surfaces compose entry points cached in SSP until refresh—call out forced refresh behaviour.
  • Deployment Agent — Reminder to activate pending security policy changes after domain edits—common miss in UAT.
  • XO MCP — Searched two way email; total 0 on SUV—toggle metadata not discoverable with that string.
  • XO MCP — risk: Ask engineering for the canonical toggle class or domain key so future gap reviews can search precisely.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Tenant-facing story is directionally right but thin on where admins click and which domains move with the toggle.
  • Recommended next step: Attach AC lines that name the exact tenant tasks and security domains impacted when the channel is flipped off.

Scenario: Disable mid-session

Given a recruiter already has the SSP compose surface open
When an admin disables two-way email
Then the UI blocks new sends with messaging consistent with HRREC-92014-style closed-channel copy.
HRREC-91979
PH: Recruiter does not have access to the compose email task		 40% — Mirrors SMS view-only precedent; XO did not return Conversation Write Access domain hits with naive search.
  • Jira — Explicitly ties Add visibility to write access on Conversation Write Access domain and REST getConversationContextInstance.
  • Salomon — HRREC-79830 SMS pattern: send UI suppressed without Modify on Candidate SMS Conversations—good analogue for acceptance language.
  • Deployment Agent — View-only is achieved by granting View without Modify on the relevant messaging domain (pattern described for SMS).
  • Jira — Recruiters with partial access might still see Reply in wide view if another story regresses—cross-check with HRREC-91982.
  • Deployment Agent — After domain tweaks, activation of pending security policy changes is required or behaviour looks flaky.
  • XO MCP — Searched Conversation Write Access; only generic relationship hits—domain not surfaced by label alone.
  • XO MCP — risk: Confirm exact domain ID and functional area name that gates email compose vs SMS.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Clear security-driven slice with SMS precedent; metadata search needs the precise domain name.
  • Recommended next step: Ask security admin to document the new email conversation domain alongside SMS domains in the rollout guide.

Scenario: View-only recruiter

Given a recruiter has only View on the conversational email domain
When they open the email task
Then Add/Reply/New stay hidden in both list and wide layouts.
HRREC-91980
PH: Recruiter sees the panel expand when clicking Add to compose an email		 33% — Narrow UI motion story; XO echoes SSP WATS coverage; low cross-lens conflict.
  • Jira — Single scenario ties Add click to expansion widths and shadow per Figma.
  • Salomon — UI Platform microtransaction references reinforce modal vs side-panel shadow differences—use to avoid double shadows.
  • Jira — Edge: double-click Add should not create duplicate compose shells—pair with HRREC-91994 send-disable behaviour.
  • XO MCP — Searched Sliding Side Panel; WATS layout and SMS/WhatsApp SSP tests returned—automation anchors only.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Small interaction story with clear Figma dependency.
  • Recommended next step: Have UX attach a short Loom for Add double-click behaviour so QA does not guess.

No additional BDD suggested—keep Jira single scenario as source of truth.
HRREC-91982
PH: Recruiter opens the message to read and reply		 45% — Combines read, reply, and disabled states; Salomon drifted to generic RTE articles; needs tighter email-thread scope in QA.
  • Jira — Wide read view plus Reply opening RTE compose; disabled conversation hides actions with explanatory copy.
  • Salomon — Many RTE/help hits are adjacent; none replace explicit email-thread disabled rules—treat as reminder to cite product not help.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-90243 excerpt (lifecycle banner timing) — consider for 2WE if "conversation closing" email copy should appear on business events, not only after first send; WhatsApp session rules do not copy to email.
  • Jira — Reply hidden states must align with HRREC-92014 terminal closure and HRREC-92006 opt-out—regression risk at intersection.
  • Salomon — Notification designer misapplication bugs show copy can mis-fire—watch placeholder text when conversation is disabled.
  • XO MCP — Searched Message Builder; convenience tasks for MB configuration—not runtime compose binding.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Important glue story between list, wide view, and compose; depends on sibling closure rules.
  • Recommended next step: Add one cross-links table in Jira listing which sibling owns each disabled reason (opt-out, bounce, close).

Scenario: Disabled thread

Given HRREC-92014 closed-conversation state
When the recruiter opens the message in wide view
Then Reply stays hidden and the info message matches the closed-reason copy deck.
HRREC-91985
PH: Recruiter can compose an email to non-agency candidate		 50% — Opt-in and domain gates are clear; XO search for Conversational Email returned generic noise; QA worries about cross-story drift.
  • Jira — Three scenarios for opt-in, not opted in, and view-only—maps cleanly to PRD language.
  • Salomon — Internal routing notes emphasise HRREC ownership of candidate messaging panel—useful for defect triage, not extra requirements.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-84390 excerpt (recruiter opt-in GWT) — consider for 2WE if recruiter-side consent mirrors WhatsApp opt-in patterns; email consent copy lives in different admin tasks.
  • Jira — Recruiters may confuse this slice with agency clone HRREC-92029—ensure scenarios name “non-agency” explicitly in UAT scripts.
  • Salomon — Blank panel after opt-out/in (HRREC-79728) suggests retesting compose visibility toggles around consent changes.
  • XO MCP — Searched Conversational Email; only generic secured relationships—no named conversational email class.
  • XO MCP — risk: Ask engineering for the class or REST resource backing opt-in checks so future searches are precise.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Consent-aware compose slice is logically sound; metadata search did not confirm backing implementation hooks.
  • Recommended next step: Pair with HRREC-92006 in refinement to align opt-out strings and disabled affordances.

Scenario: Opt-in flips while compose open

Given a candidate opts out while the recruiter is composing
When the recruiter attempts Send
Then HRREC-92004-style error path blocks send and preserves text per notes.
HRREC-91986
PH: Recruiter expands/collapses the panel to check candidate profile (context stays?)		 52% — Question mark in title reflects uncertainty; Salomon weak on draft retention; XO surfaced Conversation classes unrelated to draft state.
  • Jira — Scenario demands draft persistence across SSP collapse/expand—high value but competes with “no backend drafts” notes in discard stories.
  • Salomon — Little direct precedent; Succession replaced sliding panels with popups—do not over-interpret, but signals platform inconsistency risk.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-92062 excerpt (disable input while send in flight) — consider for 2WE if collapse during in-flight send should queue or discard partial RTE content; channel differs but UX race is similar.
  • Jira — Risk: recruiter loses RTE content when collapsing if only local state is supported—document storage boundary with HRREC-91995/91996.
  • Salomon — Kernel navigation docs may matter if collapse triggers route change vs CSS hide—QA should know which behaviour is expected.
  • XO MCP — Searched cl: Conversation; returned core Conversation classes without draft lifecycle fields in summary.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Draft persistence vs “local only” backend stance is the real tension.
  • Recommended next step: Decide with engineering whether collapse uses unmount (loses draft) or keeps mounted; update Jira title question mark accordingly.

Scenario: Collapse with unsent text

Given the recruiter typed body text and collapses the SSP without navigating away
When they expand again
Then the story states whether the draft survives and matches discard-popup expectations.
HRREC-91987
PH: Recruiter can select To, From & Subject (UI) when composing an email		 36% — UI-only header controls; Salomon gives MB admin context; XO repeats MB tasks.
  • Jira — Custom dropdowns replace native MB free-text fields per design—important scope boundary for MB team.
  • Salomon — Admin Guide MB concepts and Financials email letter example show MB can auto-populate To/From/Subject—align wording so recruiters are not confused.
  • Jira — Subject maxlength and character set not stated—pair with HRREC-91993 validations.
  • XO MCP — Searched Message Builder; configuration convenience tasks only.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Straightforward UI shell story with dependency on MB/OE list population elsewhere.
  • Recommended next step: Confirm in refinement who owns dropdown data contracts (REST field names) before VS sign-off.

No additional BDD suggested beyond Jira scenarios.
HRREC-91988
PH: Recruiter attaches documents (UI only)		 30% — Three scenarios, clear UI-only boundary; modest lens tension.
  • Jira — Covers render, attach/remove pill, and client-side type/size validation.
  • Salomon — MB admin + routing articles remind that actual allowlists may still be tenant/OE driven beyond UI mocks.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-84403 excerpt (hydrate failures must surface in composer) — consider for 2WE if attachment preview vs send could disagree; WhatsApp hydration differs from email Blobatory path.
  • Jira — Recruiters might pass UI validation but still fail server validation on Send—call out dependency on HRREC-92036.
  • XO MCP — Message Builder configuration tasks surfaced—configuration not runtime widget.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: UI-only attachment shell is well bounded; server parity is explicitly out of scope but note the handoff.
  • Recommended next step: Tag HRREC-92036 in AC as the integration point for Blobatory-backed attachments.

Scenario: Server rejects allowed UI file

Given UI accepted a file within stated limits
When Send calls REST with stricter policy
Then recruiter sees HRREC-92004 messaging without silent drop (document owner in HRREC-92036).
HRREC-91989
PH: Recruiter can use the RTE & see the UI of buttons		 28% — Explicitly excludes GenAI button; aligns with compose MVP exclusions in rule 012.
  • Jira — Scenario 2 explicitly hides GenAI sparkle—matches MVP boundary from product rules.
  • Salomon — Several GenAI-in-MB articles exist—use only to defend exclusion, not to add AI scope.
  • Jira — Risk: platform RTE upgrade reintroduces sparkle by default—add regression note for MB upgrades.
  • XO MCP — Same MB configuration hits as other compose stories.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: RTE presentation story is clear and policy-aligned.
  • Recommended next step: Add a single regression checkpoint for MB upgrades in the test plan, not new scenarios.

No additional BDD suggested.
HRREC-91990
PH: Recruiter can select From address from list of valid email addresses		 41% — Clones HRREC-92030; Salomon mixed Canvas and SMTP topics; XO weak on recruiting email string.
  • Jira — Backend-provided list including no-reply vs personal—good business framing.
  • Salomon — Default Workday SMTP From patterns appear—useful for admin comms, not always candidate reply routing.
  • Jira — If clone diverges, recruiters see inconsistent From lists between agency vs non-agency paths—keep scenarios synced.
  • XO MCP — recruiting email search returned generic relationships only.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Logical dependency story; keep clone HRREC-92030 text aligned.
  • Recommended next step: Run a quick diff review between HRREC-91990 and HRREC-92030 before sprint planning to avoid drift.

No additional BDD suggested—dedupe with clone.
HRREC-91991
PH: Recruiter does not see any tabs for non-agency candidate		 37% — Clear tab-hiding rule; Salomon adds security-domain noise; XO surfaced two-way country onboarding bindings.
  • Jira — Scenario states All/Candidate/Agency tabs hidden and defaults to single thread—simple acceptance.
  • Salomon — Candidate Notes tab security references remind that hidden tabs do not remove underlying access checks.
  • Jira — Edge: agency relationship added after apply should dynamically show tabs—confirm if in or out of scope.
  • XO MCP — two way messaging hits include onboarding method bindings and WATS mocks—country enablement, not tab logic.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: UI rule is crisp; dynamic agency association after apply may be unspecified.
  • Recommended next step: Add one sentence in Jira on whether late-bound agency linkage re-renders tabs without refresh.

Scenario: Agency linked post-apply

Given the candidate applied direct then an agency is associated
When the recruiter reopens email task
Then tabs appear or stay hidden per the clarified rule.
HRREC-91992
PH: Recruiter attaches non-supported files		 34% — Three concrete negative scenarios; Salomon rich on platform attachment limits.
  • Jira — Client-side blocks for extension, single oversize, and cumulative payload—matches enterprise expectations.
  • Salomon — REST and GraphQL attachment limit articles reinforce that tenant caps may be stricter than UI defaults—document source of truth.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-90311 excerpt (Meta marketing template US block) — consider for 2WE only as a reminder that channel-specific validation exists; do not import Meta rules—define email-specific blocked content policy separately.
  • Jira — Cumulative 30 MB example must match OE actuals—mis-stated limits become defects.
  • XO MCP — attachment search returned generic Attachment derived classes—not recruiting-specific guardrails.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Strong negative-path story; confirm the numeric limits against OE documentation.
  • Recommended next step: Ask OE for the authoritative max attachment size and count before locking QA data.

No additional BDD suggested.
HRREC-91993
PH: Recruiter sees UI error validations on Send		 32% — Inline validation for Subject/To; Salomon references other email send concurrency notes.
  • Jira — Blocking errors with aggregate count—good recruiter-visible pattern.
  • Salomon — CoffeeHub TDD notes on recruiting email vs candidate email highlight concurrency risks—optional awareness for QA pairing.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-90268 excerpt (disable Send while sending) — consider for 2WE if inline validation should coexist with disabled-send during in-flight REST; email should avoid double-send like WhatsApp composer.
  • Jira — Ensure error strings differ for To vs From vs Subject so recruiters can fix quickly.
  • XO MCP — validation returned many generic WATS “[SC]: Validation” scenarios—little recruiting specificity.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Focused validation slice with clear Given/When/Then.
  • Recommended next step: Align error copy with Message Builder standards to avoid a second tone of voice.

No additional BDD suggested.
HRREC-91994
PH: Send Message (No Attachments)		 46% — Core send path; Salomon cites SMS QA API split; XO weak on Recruiting Email; depends on HRREC-92036 clone.
  • Jira — REST dispatch, polling/retry, duplicate-send prevention—covers critical reliability themes.
  • Salomon — Two-way SMS QA material contrasts XO vs REST/Drogon—useful spike pointer if email hits similar gaps.
  • Jira — Polling story must cap backoff to avoid UI spinners stuck forever—state max wait in AC.
  • Salomon — SMS panel oddities after phone change hint environment sensitivity—run on fresh candidate emails too.
  • XO MCP — Recruiting Email search returned generic secured relationships only.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Central send story is strong but needs explicit polling limits and clone alignment to attachments.
  • Recommended next step: Add numeric polling timeout + single error path matrix shared with HRREC-92036 before hardening sprint.

Scenario: Polling timeout

Given REST accepted send but status lags
When polling exceeds the agreed timeout
Then recruiter sees a actionable message and thread shows intermediate state per design.
HRREC-91995
PH: Recruiter gets discard popup by changing pages		 44% — Navigation discard; Note says no backend drafts; Salomon kernel navigation docs; XO generic discard WATS.
  • Jira — Warns on navigation with unsaved text; aligns with May 2026 MB backend stance in Notes.
  • Salomon — Kernel navigate.modal guidance may matter if discard uses modal route vs in-panel dialog.
  • Jira — Browser refresh vs in-app navigation—ensure scenario names which loses draft.
  • XO MCP — discard hits are generic WATS discard scenarios—not recruiting-specific.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Discard-on-navigation complements HRREC-91996; boundary on refresh still fuzzy.
  • Recommended next step: Add explicit browser refresh behaviour (expect data loss vs warn) to avoid QA arguing severity.

Scenario: Browser refresh

Given unsent text in compose
When the recruiter refreshes the browser
Then Jira states whether the same discard dialog appears or data loss is accepted risk.
HRREC-91996
PH: Recruiter gets discard popup when they click the Discard button		 34% — Button and Escape parity; Salomon cites modal precedents; similar discard WATS noise in XO.
  • Jira — Distinguishes empty vs unsent text and Escape key parity—good detail.
  • Jira — Risk: Escape captured by RTE first—confirm event ordering with UX.
  • XO MCP — Discard WATS hits only.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Solid micro-interaction story tied to local-only draft stance.
  • Recommended next step: Pair test cases with HRREC-91995 so navigation and button paths cannot contradict.

No additional BDD suggested.
HRREC-91997
PH: Recruiter sees list of messages with unread ones marked in blue		 43% — Unread markers + server refresh + accessibility; Salomon mixed colour-blindness articles.
  • Jira — Scenario 3 calls out non-colour cue—important compliance hook.
  • Salomon — Unread count articles for other channels—reminder to sync iconography with SMS/WhatsApp where intentional.
  • Cross-channel (WhatsApp backlog) — HRREC-84407 excerpt (read/unread OE timing) — consider for 2WE if email “read” markers promise more than delivery receipts support; do not assume WhatsApp read semantics.
  • Jira — Concurrent read updates must define eventual consistency window—otherwise recruiters argue unread drift.
  • XO MCP — unread surfaced notifications WATS and case unread queries—adjacent domain, not recruiting thread list.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Unread list story is mostly complete; needs timing clarity on cross-recruiter read state.
  • Recommended next step: Add one AC line for refresh cadence (polling vs websocket) so QA knows how fast blue clears.

Scenario: Two recruiters

Given two recruiters view the same thread list
When one marks read
Then the other sees blue clear within the stated refresh interval.
HRREC-91998
PH: Recruiter can see message threads when clicking on a message		 38% — Thread ordering and empty reply-only threads; Salomon references unanswered conversations card.
  • Jira — Specifies descending chronological order and outbound-only threads.
  • Jira — Long threads performance not mentioned—flag as non-functional risk if transcripts can be huge.
  • XO MCP — conversation hits include WATS create/view conversation tasks—platform coverage exists.
  • Peanut — Not available this run (Jira fetch error).
  • Finding: Thread preview story is clear; performance is the latent gap.
  • Recommended next step: If transcripts can exceed a few dozen messages, add a spike line or lazy-load note in Jira.

No additional BDD suggested.
HRREC-91999
